pr.title: Fix up hardcoded pointers breaking shifts
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced multiple raw hardcoded address constructions in assembly with symbolic labels using @ha/@l or @h/@l relocations, and added missing labels/globals at data/function boundaries so those references can track address shifts. The PR was a targeted shift-resilience fix across asm/data.s, melee asm, db asm, and sysdolphin baselib asm, with no review discussion available in the dump.
what_changed: Added explicit global data labels in asm/data.s for interior table entries: lbl_8040BA5C, lbl_8040BBE8, and lbl_8040BD74.

Converted hardcoded lis/addic address pairs in asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s from numeric high halves and offsets, such as lis r3, 0x8046 plus addic. ..., -29008, to symbolic references such as lbl_80458EB0@ha and lbl_80458EB0@l.
Converted repeated DB references to lbl_804D0F90, lbl_804D0F60, and lbl_804D10B0 from raw 0x804d-based immediates to symbolic @ha/@l relocations.
Added a missing global function label func_8022F360 in asm/melee/text_3.s and changed a later reference in func_8022F3D8 to use func_8022F360@ha/@l instead of the hardcoded 0x8023/F360 address pair.
Changed function-address null checks/calls in asm/melee/text_4.s to refer symbolically to func_802F7BB4, func_802F7AF8, func_802F7C30, and func_802F7D08 rather than hardcoded 0x802f offsets.
Updated sysdolphin baselib assembly to use symbolic data labels such as lbl_8040BC3C, lbl_8040BA5C, lbl_8040BBE8, and lbl_8040BD74 instead of raw 0x8041-based address construction.
smart_moves: The fix addressed the root problem named in the PR title: hardcoded pointers do not move when code/data shifts, while label-based relocations do.
It preserved instruction forms and apparent machine-code intent by replacing only operands with equivalent symbolic relocations, minimizing behavioral risk.
It added labels exactly at previously unlabeled interior data addresses before referencing them, avoiding fake numeric constants and making the data table layout more maintainable.
It handled both data pointers and function pointers, including function-address checks that were not direct branch relocations.
It used the appropriate split-address relocation pattern for PowerPC assembly, pairing lis with @ha or @h and addi/addic with @l.
decomp_lessons: When assembly contains address construction using raw high halves and signed low offsets, convert it to label@ha/label@l or label@h/label@l as soon as the target symbol is known.
Interior data-table addresses may need their own labels if code references a subobject rather than the start of the table.
Hardcoded function addresses used for comparisons or null checks are just as shift-sensitive as hardcoded data pointers.
A successful shift fix can be very small mechanically: keep the same registers, branch structure, and instruction widths, and replace only numeric operands with relocatable symbols.
Adding .global labels before referenced asm symbols helps other files safely bind to those addresses instead of duplicating constants.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data labels use the address-derived lbl_XXXXXXXX form, e.g. lbl_8040BA5C, lbl_8040BBE8, lbl_8040BD74.
Unidentified functions use the address-derived func_XXXXXXXX form, e.g. func_8022F360 and func_802F7BB4.
PowerPC split relocations are written as symbol@ha or symbol@h for the lis operand and symbol@l for the low immediate operand.
Labels added to assembly are declared with .global immediately before the label definition when cross-file references are needed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw lis high-half immediates such as lis r3, 0x804d with lis r3, lbl_804D0F90@ha when the following low-half operation targets that symbol.
Replace signed decimal low immediates in addic/addi, such as -29008 or -17348, with the target symbol's @l relocation to maintain correct relocation behavior after shifts.
For function pointers in code, use func_XXXXXXXX@h/@l or @ha/@l instead of hardcoded text-section page constants and offsets.
Create labels at exact target addresses in data.s before converting external references to symbolic operands.
Keep comments showing original addresses/opcodes unchanged while changing assembler operands, preserving traceability in hand-written asm.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining assembly operands using raw lis 0x80xx plus addi/addic low offsets that should be converted to symbol relocations.
Search for code comparing hardcoded function addresses rather than using func_XXXXXXXX@h/@l.
Audit data.s for interior table entries referenced from code but missing explicit labels.
Check whether @h versus @ha usage is intentional for each function-address construction pattern in text_4.
